note: Tests Step 6 and Step 8 improvements (mandatory full pipeline + trace/memory artifacts). New eval: fix sleep reading bug with planted gte/lte inversion.

delta.notes: with_skill achieves 3.3x pass rate (90.9% vs 27.3%). Step 6 and Step 8 improvements are validated — agent now runs full 5-step validation pipeline, writes trace AND memory files. The only failing assertion remains delegates_to_subagent.

progress_across_iterations.note: Full workflow completed for first time
Step 6+8 improvements validated — full pipeline + artifacts

analyst_notes: HIGHEST PASS RATE EVER: 90.9% (10/11 assertions). This is the best result across all iterations.

STEP 6 FIX WORKS: Agent ran all 5 validation pipeline steps and reported results in a table — build ✓, lint-deps ✓, lint-quality ⚠, test ✓, verify ✓. This directly addresses the user's feedback from iteration 6 where E2E was skipped.

STEP 8 FIX WORKS: Agent wrote BOTH trace file (harness/trace/fix-sleep-date-range-bug.md) AND memory file (harness/memory/INDEX.md). The memory file includes procedural pattern, anti-pattern, and episodic lesson. This directly addresses the user's feedback from iteration 6.

TEST CONTAMINATION: Both agents worked on the same project directory. without_skill (195s) fixed the gte/lte inversion first. with_skill (565s) then found the code already corrected and took a more comprehensive approach (Date objects instead of strings). For future iterations, use separate project copies.

DELEGATION GAP PERSISTS: delegates_to_subagent is the ONLY failing assertion (failed in ALL 7 iterations). This may be an inherent limitation — the agent finds it more efficient to do the work directly rather than spawning sub-agents, especially for bug fixes where the scope is already well-defined.

SPEED TRADE-OFF: with_skill took 565s vs without_skill 195s — 2.9x slower. But with_skill produced: execution plan, full validation, trace file, memory file, git commit, cross-review consideration. without_skill produced: the fix and a test file. The extra time buys significant process quality.
